2605.00742.title: Position: agentic AI orchestration should be Bayes-consistent
2605.00742.paper_summary: LLMs excel at predictive tasks and complex reasoning tasks, but many high-value deployments rely on decisions under uncertainty, for example, which tool to call, which expert to consult, or how many resources to invest. While the usefulness and feasibility of Bayesian approaches remain unclear for LLM inference, this position paper argues that the control layer of an agentic AI system (that orchestrates LLMs and tools) is a clear case where Bayesian principles should shine. Bayesian decision theory provides a framework for agentic systems that can help to maintain beliefs over task-relevant latent quantities, to update these beliefs from observed agentic and human-AI interactions, and to choose actions. Making LLMs themselves explicitly Bayesian belief-updating engines remains computationally intensive and conceptually nontrivial as a general modeling target. In contrast, this paper argues that coherent decision-making requires Bayesian principles at the orchestration level of the agentic system, not necessarily the LLM agent parameters. This paper articulates practical properties for Bayesian control that fit modern agentic AI systems and human-AI collaboration, and provides concrete examples and design patterns to illustrate how calibrated beliefs and utility-aware policies can improve agentic AI orchestration.
2605.00742.paper_summary_zh: LLMs 在預測任務和複雜推理任務中表現出色，但許多高價值的應用依賴於不確定性下的決策，例如，選擇呼叫哪個工具、諮詢哪位專家或投資多少資源。雖然貝葉斯方法在 LLM 推斷中的有效性和可行性仍不明朗，但本文主張，代理 AI 系統的控制層（協調 LLM 和工具）是一個明確的案例，其中貝葉斯原則應該發揮作用。貝葉斯決策理論為代理系統提供了一個框架，可以幫助維持對任務相關潛在量的信念，從觀察到的代理和人類-AI 互動中更新這些信念，並選擇行動。使 LLM 本身明確成為貝葉斯信念更新引擎仍然在計算上是密集的，並且作為一般建模目標在概念上並不簡單。相比之下，本文主張，連貫的決策需要在代理系統的協調層面上應用貝葉斯原則，而不一定是 LLM 代理參數。本文闡明了適合現代代理 AI 系統和人類-AI 協作的貝葉斯控制的實用特性，並提供具體示例和設計模式，以說明如何通過校準的信念和考慮效用的政策來改善代理 AI 的協調。
2605.00742.author: Theodore Papamarkou et.al.
2605.00742.authors: Theodore Papamarkou, Pierre Alquier, Matthias Bauer, Wray Buntine, Andrew Davison, Gintare Karolina Dziugaite, Maurizio Filippone, Andrew Y. K.
